history: The client has a master's degree in engineering and experience in robotics. She has a history of remorse over not dedicating enough time to past projects, which amplifies her current feelings of uncertainty about her career transition. She was diagnosed with ADHD in elementary school and has a history of self-harm, though she hasn't engaged in it for years. She experiences emotional regulation issues, which are partially managed by medication.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
intermediate_belief: If I don't make the right career choice, then I will fail and be left behind by my peers.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't find fulfillment in my career, then I will always feel inadequate and unhappy.
coping_strategies: The client tends to stay busy all the time to avoid feelings of boredom and restlessness. She also struggles with nail-biting and experiences difficulty concentrating due to distractibility.
situation: Considering launching a micro press and changing careers. Feeling uncertain about financial stability and fulfillment while comparing self to peers.
auto_thought: I might fail at this new venture, and everyone will see me as a failure.
behavior: She compares herself to her peers, feels scattered, and remains on guard all the time.
